original_hypothesis.title: Gauge genesis deposits can retroactively steal past rewards due to missing per-user reward checkpointing
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeV2.depositsForGenesis updates global reward state with updateReward(address(0)) but does not checkpoint the beneficiary’s userRewardPerTokenPaid. If rewards were already accruing, a genesis depositor can later call getReward() and receive rewards as if they had been staked since the beginning, draining rewards allocated to earlier stakers.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a direct invariant violation (INV-STATE-001). Although only genesisPool can call depositsForGenesis (so attacker must be a genesis participant and the genesis system must trigger deposits after rewards started), this is a realistic operational sequencing bug during launches/epochs and can cause immediate reward insolvency or unfair distribution.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _depositsForGenesis uses updateReward(address(0)) and never sets userRewardPerTokenPaid[tokenOwner] to rewardPerTokenStored

preconditions: Gauge has non-zero rewardPerTokenStored from prior distribution periods (notifyRewardAmount called earlier)
GenesisPool calls depositsForGenesis for a user after rewards started accruing
User later calls getReward() (public) or distribution calls getReward(user)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Genesis stake accounting is split across gauge and genesisPool; per-user reward checkpointing omitted
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires genesisPool action sequencing; not strictly admin-only once genesis runs
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Whenever genesis deposits occur after any rewards accrual

operational_questions: Can notifyRewardAmount be called before genesis deposits complete in production workflows?
Does genesis launch pipeline guarantee deposits happen before any emissions begin?
Is genesisPool trusted to call depositsForGenesis only in a safe window?
validation_requirements: Simulate reward distribution, then call depositsForGenesis, then claim rewards; quantify retroactive reward capture
Review genesis manager scripts/contracts (out of provided scope) for ordering guarantees
Check if emergency mode or epoch flips can cause deposits and rewards to overlap

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Ensure gauge has accrued rewards (wait for distribution event)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Participate in genesis such that genesisPool deposits stake after rewards started
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Call getReward() to harvest retroactive rewards
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Repeat across multiple genesis beneficiaries if possible
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Depends on genesis timing; on-chain claim is unilateral

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Reward pool drained; legitimate stakers underpaid; governance disputes
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High (may require reimbursements and/or contract migration)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Gauge emissions distribution fairness and solvency

execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: Medium (must align with genesis deposit timing)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Genesis deposits into gauge after notifyRewardAmount events; unusually large first-time claims
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: RewardToken balance drops faster than expected post-genesis
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Genesis addresses claiming outsized rewards immediately after first interaction

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
